title: vicia tenuifolia subsp. villosa
intro: Vicia tenuifolia subsp. villosa, commonly known as hairy vetch or hairy tare, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Vicia tenuifolia subsp. villosa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea, infused in water, or used as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
